title: DAPI reconstitution and viability staining
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bkv0c","text":"For the reconstitution of DAPI to make a stock solution and dilution for a working solution.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fr20i","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cgila","text":"Details of viability staining volumes also provided. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Add 2ml of water to the 10mg vial of DAPI to make a 14.3mM (5mg/mL) stock solution. Vortexing may be required if the powder does not dissolve with pipetting. (Note: DAPI will not dissolve in PBS) Add a label with the date of reconstitution and store this stock solution at 4 degrees C for 6 months.
Add 420uL DAPI stock to 20mL PBS to make a working solution of 300uM. Store in the dark at 4 degrees C.

For immunofluorescence, add to fixed cells at a concentration of 300nM (i.e. 1:1000 dilution for 5 mins then wash cells in PBS 3 times. For flow cytometry, add to live cells at a concentration of 3mM (i.e. 1:100)
creator: Emily Stephenson
